title: Structured, scannable agent status lines with progressive disclosure
source: CLI UX guidance (Command Line Interface Guidelines / clig.dev) — human-first output, alignment, and progressive disclosure of detail
description: Delegate/team output benefits from aligned, columnar status lines (agent name, role, ticket, state, elapsed) with a compact default view and a verbose flag for full detail, following clig.dev's 'human-readable by default, greppable when needed' principle. Consistent alignment and a stable glyph prefix per state (queued/running/done/failed) makes multi-Morty runs far easier to scan in the terminal and mirrors the GUI's row layout.
proposed_changes: Add a `format_status_row(agent, role, ticket, state, elapsed)` helper in visual.py that pads columns to fixed widths and prefixes a state glyph (○ queued, ◐ running, ● done, ✖ failed). Have delegate.py emit these rows for each active Morty and re-render in place under a TTY. Add a `--verbose/-v` flag that appends the tool-call summary; keep the default one-line-per-agent view greppable (stable field order, no color codes when piped).
